{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''Jedi''. If…you…''fail''…will you… help us…make ''war''…against the ''dark side''?|Drokko Kira, to Ulic Qel-Droma|<ref name="beastwars" />}}
'''Drokko Kira''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], the father of [[Beast-Lord]] [[Modon Kira]], grandfather of [[Oron Kira]], and a native of the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Around [[4050 BBY]], Kira challenged the legacy of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Freedon Nadd]], who had crowned himself [[King/Legends|King]] of Onderon [[4400 BBY|centuries before]] and taught his descendants the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. For this, Kira was banished from Iziz, sustaining an unhealing wound in the process. Kira did not speak again for fifty years but eventually joined the [[Beast Riders]], a group of criminals cast out of Iziz who waged a [[Beast Wars|war]] on the capital. In [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], Kira attended the [[Binding Ceremony of Oron Kira and Galia|binding ceremony]] of his grandson, Oron, and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Galia]], heiress to the Onderonian throne. The ceremony was interrupted by three [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]—brothers [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]] and [[Cay Qel-Droma]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Tott Doneeta]]—whom Modon enlisted to fight the dark side reign in Iziz. Although Ulic Qel-Droma wanted to lead a peace mission, Drokko Kira, speaking for the first time in years, asked him if he would fight alongside the Beast Riders if the mission failed; Qel-Droma agreed. The peace talks indeed proved unsuccessful and the Beast Riders were victorious in the ensuing [[Battle of Iziz|showdown]] in Iziz.

==Biography==
{{Quote|My grandfather ''Drokko'', who you see sitting silently before you, was cast out of Iziz fifty years ago—''because he challenged the dark side!''|Oron Kira|<ref name="beastwars" />}}
Drokko Kira was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Kira was a native of the walled [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] and lived during a period when the city's [[Iziz ruling family|royal family]] was constantly [[Beast Wars|warring]] with outcast [[Beast Riders]] who dwelt beyond Iziz's walls. In [[4400 BBY]], a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|fallen]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]–turned–[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] named [[Freedon Nadd]] came to Onderon and subjugated the [[Planet/Legends|world]], crowning himself [[King/Legends|king]] and eventually teaching his descendants the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Around [[4050 BBY]], Kira challenged the legacy of Nadd. For this, he was branded as a criminal and cast out of Iziz. Kira suffered an unhealing wound during this time and did not speak again for fifty years. Kira soon joined the Beast Riders and eventually had a son, [[Modon Kira]], who became a respected and powerful [[Beast-Lord]]. Modon, in turn, had a son, named [[Oron Kira]]. Oron eventually fell in love with [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Galia]], heiress to the Onderonian throne. Galia returned his affections and, in [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], she came to [[Kira Fortress|Fortress Kira]] to marry him.<ref name="beastwars" />

Drokko Kira, along with Modon, attended the [[Binding Ceremony of Oron Kira and Galia]]. However, the festivities were interrupted by the arrival of three [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]—brothers [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]] and [[Cay Qel-Droma]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Tott Doneeta]]—who had been sent by Onderonian [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]] to retrieve Galia. They unhanded Galia when she informed them that she was there willingly and that they were welcomed to the banquet by Modon. Oron told the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] about the dark side leanings of the Onderonian monarchy, and Modon asked them to join the fight against them. Ulic Qel-Droma preferred to resolve the conflict peacefully and proposed a mission to do just that. Suddenly, Drokko Kira emerged from his stupor and spoke for the first time in decades, asking Qel-Droma if he would join the fight against the dark side should the peace mission fail, and Qel-Droma agreed. The peace mission was a failure, and Modon's [[Beast Rider army]] defeated Queen Amanoa's forces in the deciding [[Battle of Iziz]], ending the reign of the dark side on Onderon.<ref name="beastwars" />

==Personality and traits==
In his younger years, Drokko Kira was considerably bolder, openly challenging the legacy of Freedon Nadd that gripped Onderon. However, after his banishment, Kira became locked in a stupor, not speaking a word for fifty years. Yet, when talk of bringing down the dark side–worshiping monarchy in Iziz once again surrounded him, Kira perked up and spoke for the first time since his banishment.<ref name="beastwars" />

==Behind the scenes==
Drokko Kira first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon]]'', the first story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released in [[1993]]. Kira appears in the [[Tales of the Jedi 2|second and final issue]] of the arc, playing a relatively minor role. In this appearance he is illustrated by [[Chris Gossett]].<ref name="beastwars" /> The [[Star Wars Insider 26|''Star Wars Insider'' 26]] article [[The Horse's Mouth|''Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi Universe, Part 1'']] says that Kira was cast out of Iziz at 4048 BBY,<ref name="insider">{{InsiderCite|26|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 1}}</ref> while the ''Tales of the Jedi'' comic places his exile at 4,050 BBY.<ref name="beastwars" /> As ''Insider 26'' contradicts many other established dates,<ref name="insider" /> this article treats the comic as being correct.
